Ford Transit Custom Automatic The Ford Transit Custom automatic stands as the UK’s best-selling vehicle, surpassing many passenger cars in. This commercial workhorse has transformed into a versatile powerhouse…

ouse that exceeds typical van expectations. This piece provides comprehensive details about the automatic Ford Transit Custom, whether you’re looking to buy one or need specifications for your fleet. [Source](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Ford_Transit_Custom)  
These vans have earned businesses’ trust through their proven performance. The Ford Transit Custom automatic delivers serious capability with payload capacities fromkg to,384kg and can tow up to,800kg, while providing car-like comfort. The-inch touchscreen infotainment system makes it one of the most advanced vans in its class. ](https://www.vansales.com/)

The latest lineup features multiple configurations that include panel van, crew cab, and the innovative Multicab variant. The Multicab can transport up to five people without compromising loading length. The PHEV model achieves fuel efficiency up to.6mpg, and load volumes range between.0 and.3 cubic meters. These options let us direct you toward the perfect Transit Custom that matches your needs. ](https://www.vansales.com/product-category/van-sales/ford-vans-for-sale/ford-transit-custom-for-sale/)

## Ford Transit Custom Automatic: Specs, Trims and Engine Options

The Ford Transit Custom lineup gives you plenty of powertrain options that work for any business need. I’ve looked at the entire range, and Ford clearly focused on flexibility while you retain the Transit’s proven capability. See all Ford Van Lease deals

### Engine Choices:.0L Diesel, PHEV, and Electric

The.0-liter EcoBlue diesel engine remains at the heart of the Transit Custom range. You can choose from power outputs between bhp to bhp. Businesses looking to cut emissions without losing range will find the plug-in hybrid (PHEV) a great option. It pairs a.0-liter EcoBoost petrol engine with an electric motor to deliver bhp combined output. This hybrid setup lets you drive up to miles on electric power alone – perfect for city deliveries or when you operate in low emission zones. See New Ford Transit Custom

The E-Transit Custom serves businesses ready for full electric power. It comes with either akW or more powerfulkW electric motor and runs up to miles on a single charge. The electric version still handles heavy loads with a payload capacity of,327 kg and maximum load volume of.8 cubic meters.

### Trim Levels: Leader, Trend, Limited, Sport, Trail

The Transit Custom comes in several well-laid-out trim levels:

-   **Leader**: This entry-level trim (replacing the previous “Base” model) has central locking,-inch steel wheels, side loading door, and a simple.2-inch TFT display with DAB radio.
-   **Trend**: You get body-colored bumpers and mirrors, plastic wheel trims, an-inch touchscreen,-way adjustable driver’s seat, and cruise control.
-   **Limited**: Steps up with chrome grille surround, body-colored side moldings,-inch alloy wheels, heated seats, and air conditioning.
-   **Sport**: This top trim features an athletic styling kit with lower front bumper skirt, side stripes with Transit branding, body-colored side skirts, and-inch alloy wheels.
-   **Trail**: Built specifically for off-road use.

### Transmission:-Speed Manual vs-Speed Automatic

A six-speed manual transmission comes standard across the Transit Custom range. Ford now offers a smooth eight-speed automatic transmission if you want more convenience. The automatic option starts from thehp diesel engine and works great in stop-start city traffic. The PHEV variant comes with the automatic gearbox as standard, making a perfect match.

### Body Styles: Panel Van, Crew Cab, Kombi, Tourneo

The Transit Custom’s versatility shines through its multiple body options. You can get the panel van in two lengths (L1 and L2) and two roof heights. The crew cab version adds extra seats while keeping cargo space, and Ford’s new MultiCab cleverly fits five seats without sacrificing loading length.

The Kombi serves as a simple people carrier, while the Tourneo Custom takes comfort to a higher level. Each version keeps the Transit’s core strengths – excellent cargo space, practical loading features, and driver-friendly design that made this model such a success.

## Driving Experience: How the Automatic Feels on the Road

The Ford Transit Custom automatic impressed me right away. Its refined, car-like handling combined with van capabilities makes it stand out. The auto-shifter mounted on the steering column creates a cleaner cabin that’s easy to move through.

### Steering and Suspension Feedback

This Transit Custom comes with light, precise steering that makes handling this big van feel easy. Drivers won’t get as tired during long workdays thanks to these responsive controls. The model focuses on user-friendliness without losing accuracy, unlike its predecessor’s firmer steering feel.

Ford has made the suspension better by a lot. The van stays firmly planted on the road with its independent rear suspension. You won’t experience the bouncing that was common in older models. The setup handles bumps really well, giving you great comfort for a work vehicle. The front wheels sit slightly forward now, which helps the Transit Custom drive more like a car.

We tested the suspension with about half a ton of cargo. It smoothed out road bumps nicely while keeping the van stable. The van handles confidently even with heavy loads.

### Urban vs Highway Driving

The Transit Custom really shines in city streets. Its tight turning radius and quick controls make it easy to handle busy roads. The automatic transmission proves its worth during city deliveries, and the Delivery Assist feature takes care of security tasks to reduce driver workload.

Highway driving is just as good. The-speed automatic has a higher top gear than the manual version, which means quieter and more efficient highway cruising. You’ll save fuel on long trips, though city driving might use a bit more gas.

The van offers six drive modes: Normal, Eco, Sport, Slippery Road, Tow/Haul, and Trail. Each mode changes how the throttle, steering, and gear shifts work to give you the best performance in any situation.

### Noise and Cabin Refinement

The automatic Transit Custom keeps things quiet inside. Road noise, engine sounds, and wind barely get through, especially at higher speeds. The large mirrors have smart designs that cut down wind noise.

Diesel versions have a slight engine rumble, but it won’t bother you. The wall between the cab and cargo area blocks most noise from the back, though some rattles might come through.

Electric and PHEV models give you the quietest ride. The PHEV switches smoothly between electric and petrol power.

### Automatic Gearbox Responsiveness

The-speed automatic shifts gears smoothly in all driving conditions. It works great with diesel engines fromhp and up. The PS.0-liter EcoBlue engine combo gives you power exactly when you need it.

Ford has tested this transmission for over million kilometers to make sure it lasts, even with heavy commercial use. You won’t need to push the throttle as hard to get good performance, which helps reduce fatigue during delivery runs.

The automatic transmission handles towing up to,500kg with no problems. Tow/Haul mode adjusts gear shifts to optimize performance with heavy loads.

Models with AWD keep their grip on rough terrain and corner better, even with standard tires. Power automatically goes to the wheels with the most grip, which helps stability on unpaved roads, gravel, and in bad weather.

## Interior and Tech: Comfort, Storage and Infotainment

The Ford Transit Custom automatic welcomes you with an interior that combines practicality and unexpected refinement. The cabin design creates a perfect balance between comfort and functionality as a mobile office.

### Cabin Layout and Seating Comfort

The seats feature ergonomic design with proper lumbar support to help drivers stay fresh on extended trips. Premium trims come with heated seats that make winter driving more comfortable. The new flat floor design makes a big deal as it means that middle-seat passengers have more space than before. Drivers can also slide across to exit through the passenger door when needed. The interior feels more like a car thanks to premium materials, and better sound insulation keeps road noise at bay.

###-Inch Touchscreen and SYNC Features

A stunning-inch infotainment touchscreen dominates the dashboard on every model. Ford’s next-generation SYNC system includes:

-   Cloud-connected navigation with up-to-the-minute traffic and weather data
-   Advanced voice recognition with conversational commands
-   Wireless smartphone integration with Apple CarPlay and Android Auto
-   Over-the-air updates to keep systems current

The standardG modem keeps you connected even at remote work locations. An-inch digital driver’s display shows essential information about driver assistance systems and trip details right in front of you.

### Storage Compartments and Flat Floor Design

Smart storage solutions make the Transit Custom stand out. Ford created extra storage in the instrument panel by moving the passenger airbag to the roof. The van’s under-seat storage fits laptops and files with ease. A column-mounted auto-shifter and electronic parking brake create more space and allow smooth movement through the cabin. These updates make it easier to move around different areas of the cab.

### Optional Features: Folding Steering Table, Digital Mirror

The unique “squircle” steering wheel in the Cab as Office Pack turns into a tilting table while parked. This feature lets you work on your laptop or enjoy lunch breaks comfortably. The van becomes your office on wheels. The digital rear-view mirror is a clever addition that provides clear views behind the van, solving visibility issues in models with fixed bulkheads. Combined with the-degree camera system, parking and maneuvering in tight spaces becomes easier than ever.

## Load Space, Payload and Towing Capacity

The Ford Transit Custom automatic’s cargo capabilities remain a vital factor when business owners make their decision. You’ll find multiple configurations available, and knowing the exact specifications will help you select the right van that meets your needs.

### Cargo Volume by Model (L1/L2, H1/H2)

The Transit Custom gives you versatile load space options throughout its range. The short wheelbase low roof (L1H1) configuration provides.8 cubic meters of cargo volume. This space grows to.8 cubic meters in the long wheelbase low roof (L2H1) variant. Businesses that need maximum vertical space will appreciate the high roof models. These offer even more capacity—6.6 cubic meters for short wheelbase high roof (L1H2) and.7 cubic meters for long wheelbase high roof (L2H2) configurations.

The interior dimensions change based on the model. L1 models give you a maximum load length of,602mm, while L2 versions stretch to,002mm. The load-through hatch extends these measurements to,050mm and,450mm respectively. You’ll get a maximum interior width of,777mm with,392mm between wheel arches.

### Payload Range:kg to,406kg

The Transit Custom’s payload capacity links directly to its gross vehicle weight ratings. You’ll find three main weight categories—280 (2,825kg GVW), (3,025kg GVW), and (3,225kg GVW). The payload capacity ranges fromkg in the lightest variants to,416kg in the models. Most businesses choose the middle-range series that offers payloads between,048kg and,178kg.

### Towing Capacity: Up to,800kg

The automatic Ford Transit Custom delivers excellent towing capabilities. The most capable version—thehp Trend in L1H1 form—can tow up to,800kg. Other configurations still pull impressive loads around,300kg. The plug-in hybrid variant maintains a,300kg towing capacity, which sets it apart from other alternative powertrain vehicles that usually compromise on towing ability.

### Multicab and Double Cab Configurations

The sort of thing I love about Ford’s “Multicab” configuration is its smart design. This setup uses an L-shaped bulkhead that fits up to five people while keeping the full loading length through a narrow passage beside the rear seats. The Double Cab in-van (DCiV) setup seats six passengers with a smaller load space of,605mm (L1) or,004mm (L2) to the bulkhead.

The Transit Custom automatic gives you exceptional versatility across its range. It balances payload capacity, cargo volume, and people-carrying abilities to meet the needs of businesses of all sizes.

## Running Costs, Reliability and Common Issues

Business owners need to know the Ford Transit Custom automatic’s running costs and reliability. Let’s get into what you can expect from daily ownership.

### Fuel Economy: Diesel vs PHEV

The.0-liter diesel engines give reasonable efficiency. Official WLTP figures range frommpg (for thebhp automatic with four-wheel drive) to.9mpg (for thebhp manual front-wheel drive). Most diesel variants stay aroundmpg. This matches my real-life driving experience with lighter loads.

The PHEV model claimsmpg on paper. My experience shows figures abovempg after a full battery charge. The numbers drop to mid-30s mpg once the battery runs out. You’ll get up to miles of electric-only range. A.5kW home charger takes about four hours to charge fully.

### Service Intervals and Warranty

Ford wants you to service your Transit Custom every two years or,000 miles. This schedule helps keep your van reliable with minimal downtime. The van comes with an Intelligent Oil-Life Monitor that checks engine oil condition and tells you when it needs service.

The warranty lasts three years or,000 miles. This falls behind competitors like Renault, Vauxhall, and Volkswagen that give three years/100,000 miles. Nissan leads the pack with five years/100,000 miles coverage.

### Known Gearbox Problems in Automatic Models

The automatic transmission has some reported problems. Owners notice shuddering or juddering when moving from a stop, which shows possible clutch assembly faults. Harsh thudding during gear changes points to valve body issues. Poor maintenance can lead to transmission slipping or complete failure.

### Real-Life Owner Feedback

Many Transit Custom owners say their vans serve them well with good maintenance. Transit series vans usually last-15 years and can reach,000 miles. Regular maintenance is vital. Oil change timing matters a lot, especially with frequent short trips, city driving, or heavy loads.

## Conclusion

My time with the Ford Transit Custom automatic shows why it’s the UK’s best-selling vehicle. This van works great as a commercial vehicle and drives like a car without losing any capability. Businesses can choose from various powertrains – from fuel-saving diesels to a cutting-edge PHEV that deliversmpg – to match their exact needs.

The cabin’s new design stands out as the biggest improvement from older models. A-inch touchscreen with SYNC technology turns this van into a proper mobile office. Smart storage options make daily work much easier. The automatic transmission makes driving smoother, especially during city deliveries where the-speed gearbox really proves its worth.

The Transit Custom automatic keeps running costs reasonable with maintenance needed every two years or,000 miles, though some users report transmission problems. Its,406kg payload capacity and ability to tow,800kg prove it’s a versatile commercial vehicle.

Ford has hit the sweet spot between practicality, comfort, and technology in the Transit Custom automatic. The new Multicab setup and digital rear-view mirror show Ford knows what businesses need from their vans. While other manufacturers might give better warranty coverage, few vans can match the Transit Custom’s mix of capabilities, driving feel, and tech features.

## FAQs

**Q1. How does the automatic transmission perform in the Ford Transit Custom?** The-speed automatic transmission in the Ford Transit Custom offers smooth gear changes and responsive performance. It’s well-matched with diesel engines fromhp upward and provides a more relaxed driving experience, especially during highway cruising and urban multi-drop deliveries.

**Q2. What are the payload and towing capacities of the Ford Transit Custom automatic?** The Ford Transit Custom automatic offers payload capacities ranging fromkg to,416kg, depending on the model. Towing capacity reaches up to,800kg for the most capable versions, with most configurations able to tow around,300kg.

**Q3. How fuel-efficient is the Ford Transit Custom automatic?** Fuel efficiency varies by engine type. Diesel variants typically achieve aroundmpg in real-world conditions. The PHEV model claims up tompg on paper, though real-world performance depends on battery charge and driving conditions, with figures often exceedingmpg when fully charged.

**Q4. What advanced features are available in the Ford Transit Custom’s interior?** The model features a-inch touchscreen with SYNC technology, offering cloud-connected navigation and wireless smartphone integration. Optional features include a folding steering wheel table for mobile office use and a digital rear-view mirror for improved visibility.

**Q5. Are there any common issues with the Ford Transit Custom automatic?** Some owners have reported transmission-related problems, including shuddering when moving from a standstill and harsh shifting. However, with proper maintenance, many Transit Custom owners experience reliable service, with the potential for a-15 year lifespan and up to,000 miles of use.
